summary: The P1 spine epic 4smp (content variants) gates its whole child set on read-algebra beads that sit below it: 4p1 (P3 decision) and jnj.1 (P3) are load-bearing for rlsb, while fnm.2/6/10 are already P2. But the 'one read algebra' is not actually undecided — it is already realized in code as QueryProjectionSpec = SelectionSpec x ProjectionSpec x RenderSpec (polylogue/surfaces/projection_spec.py); 4p1's remaining work is to RECORD that decision + inventory conformance, not to make a new choice. Resolution is hybrid: record+lift 4p1 and lift jnj.1 to P2 so the algebra frontier is buildable, and resequence 4smp so the substrate children (arso, 0v9p) proceed now while the algebra-dependent children (rlsb, then d4zk) gate behind that frontier.

reprioritizations.why: 4p1 is a decision that blocks the P1 spine epic 4smp through its child rlsb ('Extend the existing Query x Projection x Render algebra... Coordinate with ...4p1'). A P1 epic cannot land coherently behind a P3 decision. The decision is cheap because it is already realized in code (projection_spec.py: 'This module names the algebra that should replace view-specific flag clusters'); the deliverable is a doc entry in docs/architecture-spine.md + a conformance inventory, not a rewrite. P2 places it on the buildable frontier ahead of the P1 work without over-weighting a doc/inventory bead to P1.

jnj.1 owns the projection/render duplication convergence (ProjectionSpec.body_policy/exclude_block_kinds vs ContentProjectionSpec; RenderFormat vs SESSION_OUTPUT_FORMATS; RenderDestination vs ReadViewInvocation; RenderSpec.layout vs read-view/profile metadata). rlsb (P1 child of 4smp) explicitly requires that same convergence ('audit and converge the existing selection/projection/render duplication... Coordinate with ...jnj.1') before it adds variant_policy to ProjectionSpec, or variants accrete yet another surface. Leaving the convergence at P3 under a P1 dependent is a priority inversion; lift to P2 to match the fnm DSL stages it sits beside.

decision_4p1.set_design: RESOLVE-BY-RECORD, not open-design. The one read algebra already exists in code and is the answer: polylogue/surfaces/projection_spec.py defines QueryProjectionSpec = SelectionSpec x ProjectionSpec x RenderSpec. SelectionSpec = which evidence rows (refs/query/origin/since/until/project_path/limit); ProjectionSpec = which evidence families + body policy + fields (EvidenceFamily enum: sessions/messages/blocks/actions/raw/context/chronicle/neighbors/correlation/temporal/assertions; BodyPolicy: full/omit-tool-outputs/authored-dialogue/metadata-only); RenderSpec = encoding/delivery only (RenderFormat, RenderDestination, layout id, timestamp policy, out). Named presets already exist: READ_VIEW_PROJECTION_FAMILIES and NAMED_PROJECTION_FAMILIES map view names to (P) family tuples. So 4p1 is a DECISION whose deliverable is: (1) Record it as a Major Decision in docs/architecture-spine.md (the file currently has no read-algebra entry under 'Major Decisions') stating the three spec types, their composition law (a preset = a named (S,P,R) triple registered via the declare-once machinery), and the rule that MCP read tools / analyze modes / CLI views / web routes / read-view profiles are NAMED PRESETS over the algebra, never new code paths. (2) Conformance inventory: classify every read surface (CLI verbs + read --view profiles, analyze boolean modes, ~61 MCP read tools, daemon web routes, read-view profiles, read-package layouts) as conformant / preset-expressible / algebra-hole / genuinely-other; the algebra-holes become the fnm/jnj priority list. (3) Record the current duplication as explicit holes with owning beads: ProjectionSpec.body_policy/exclude_block_kinds vs ContentProjectionSpec (polylogue/archive/semantic/content_projection.py) -> jnj.1/rlsb; RenderFormat vs SESSION_OUTPUT_FORMATS; RenderDestination vs ReadViewInvocation.destination/deliver_content; RenderSpec.layout free-string vs read-view/profile metadata; READ_VIEW_PROJECTION_FAMILIES vs READ_VIEW_PROFILES vs executable handlers. (4) Scope guard: writes, ops, and maintenance flows are NOT in this algebra; do not force-fit them (semantic inclusion lives in ProjectionSpec/ContentProjectionSpec, delivery/encoding lives in RenderSpec).
decision_4p1.set_acceptance: docs/architecture-spine.md gains a 'One read algebra' entry under Major Decisions naming SelectionSpec x ProjectionSpec x RenderSpec (QueryProjectionSpec) as the sole read contract and presets as named (S,P,R) triples, with projection_spec.py cited as the existing realization. A conformance inventory (table in the doc or a linked docs/plans/*.yaml manifest checked by devtools verify manifests) lists every read surface with its classification; every algebra-hole row links to the fnm or jnj bead that closes it. The expansion policy is stated as doctrine: a new read affordance (new MCP tool, analyze mode, web panel, static HTML/variant/translation view) must be expressible as a preset/spec value, and if it needs a bespoke read path the algebra gains or reuses the capability FIRST. The writes/ops/maintenance scope guard is recorded. This bead requires no code rewrite; it references the existing spec types and hands the enumerated holes to fnm/jnj/rlsb. devtools render all --check passes after the doc edit.
decision_4p1.recommended_resolution: The one read algebra IS SelectionSpec x ProjectionSpec x RenderSpec (QueryProjectionSpec) and it is ALREADY DECIDED in code (projection_spec.py) — no alternative is under consideration. 4p1 should therefore be reframed from 'undecided decision' to 'record + inventory' and lifted to P2, then done first so rlsb/jnj.1/fnm build against a stated invariant instead of rediscovering the direction. Concretely: write the Major-Decision entry + conformance inventory, enumerate the duplication holes as fnm/jnj work, then rlsb extends ProjectionSpec with variant_policy (never a new overlay). This unblocks the P1 4smp epic without inventing new architecture.

resequence_notes: Split the four 4smp children into a substrate frontier and an algebra-dependent tail rather than blocking the whole epic on the read-algebra decision. FRONTIER (proceed now, no algebra-decision gate): (1) polylogue-arso — the variant substrate is typed models + storage DDL + repository methods + variant:/variant-node: ref resolution + alignment edges; none of that requires 4p1 recorded or the projection duplication converged, so arso should be marked ready and not gated on 4p1. (2) polylogue-0v9p — language detection facts are an independent derived-fact layer and a prerequisite for choosing variant translation targets; also ungated. GATED TAIL (behind the algebra frontier): (3) polylogue-rlsb — lives entirely inside the algebra ('Extend the existing Query x Projection x Render algebra... add a ProjectionSpec variant_policy'), so it should hard-depend (blocks, not the current relates-to) on 4p1 (recorded doctrine) and jnj.1 (duplication convergence), and consumes fnm.2/6/10 projection stages; it also needs arso's substrate. (4) polylogue-d4zk — UX over rlsb's projection surface plus bby.11 (webui v2) and s7ae.3 (coordination messages); depends-on rlsb and should stay last. DEP EDGES TO SET: rlsb depends-on {arso, 4p1, jnj.1}; d4zk depends-on {rlsb, s7ae.3}; leave arso and 0v9p with no algebra-decision blocker so the epic starts immediately. Keep all four children at P1 (they are spine); the fix is sequencing via deps + lifting the two blocking read-algebra beads (4p1, jnj.1) to P2, not down-prioritizing the children. Note the epic's own dependency on 4p1 is currently 'relates-to' (soft); the real blocking edge is 4p1 -> rlsb, so the epic-level relate can stay while rlsb gains the hard dep.
